page: Microsoft Azure D48s_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL x86_64 processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The processor includes a 35 MB L3 cache and supports hardware flags such as AVX-512 and AES cryptography. The instance is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks place the D48s_v4 in the average range for most compute and memory tasks. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng registers at 43994.28, and the Geekbench multi-core score is 12037.0. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show strong performance for large block read-only and write operations, though small block cached reads remain in the average tier. A key performance tradeoff is observed in large language model inference; while prompt processing speeds are average, text generation speeds for 135M and 7B models are weak, and 70B model prompt processing is also weak, indicating limitations for real-time AI generation workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D48s_v4 offers a balanced resource density suitable for enterprise workloads that require substantial CPU and memory capacity without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The absence of local storage means storage costs are decoupled, allowing users to scale Premium Storage independently. This configuration is suitable for general-purpose workloads, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation.
description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv4 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL x86_64 processor with 48 vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM. Operating at 2.6 GHz, it features AVX-512 support and a 35 MB L3 cache. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks indicate average multi-core CPU and database performance, alongside strong large-block memory bandwidth. However, it exhibits weak text generation speeds in LLM inference benchmarks. This server is suitable for general-purpose enterprise applications, software compilation, and database workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and Premium Storage capability, delivering balanced compute and memory performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and Premium Storage capability for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high memory bandwidth, and flexible external storage options.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio.
Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with AVX-512 vector extensions.
Strong memory bandwidth for large block read and write operations.
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage.
Suitable for general-purpose enterprise applications, database operations, and software compilation.
